items.title: Disney Dreamlight Valley Guide: Tweedle Dee and Tweedle Dum Motif Locations
items.summary: Find all 4 Tweedle Dee and Tweedle Dum Motifs in DDV's Alice in Wonderland Realm. Complete Alice's Picture Pursuit quest with this step-by-step guide.
items.content:
The Picture Pursuit: Alice quest earns its reputation as one of the trickier assignments in Disney Dreamlight Valley 's ongoing Picture Pursuit event. Alice sends you into her massive Alice in Wonderland Realm to hunt down four Tweedle Dee and Tweedle Dum Motifs , and the sheer size of that area combined with how small these Motifs appear against all the colorful scenery makes it genuinely easy to walk right past them.

The good news: every single Motif is confined to the first section of the Realm. You never need to cross into the Queen of Hearts' castle territory. Work through the four locations below in order and you'll be back to Alice in under ten minutes.

Before heading into the Realm, you need to talk to Alice first. The quest is tied directly to her, so the Motifs won't register until you've accepted it from her in conversation. Once that's done, head to the Alice in Wonderland Realm either through the Dream Castle door entrance or by using the fast travel option on your map to teleport there instantly.

tip Fast travel is the quicker option here. Use your map to jump straight into the Realm rather than navigating through the Dream Castle. Motif 4 on the tree portal

For a broader look at every quest and reward tied to this event, the Picture Pursuit event quests and rewards guide covers the full scope of what's available before the event closes.

All four Motifs sit within the first section of the Realm. None of them require you to use the pink mushroom teleport to reach the Queen's castle area, so stay in the forest section throughout.

From your spawn point, walk forward just a few steps. The cooking station will appear on your left almost immediately. Look behind the flowers clustered near it and you'll spot the first Motif tucked there. This one is the easiest of the four to grab since it's essentially right where you land.

This Motif sits almost directly across from the first one. From your spawn point, look to the right to find a pond , then follow the edge of it toward the waterfall. The Motif is on the left side of the waterfall wall . If the Realm's busy visual style is making it hard to pick out, use the pond as your landmark and scan upward along the waterfall's left edge.

Head to the back right corner of the first Realm section. You'll find a pink mushroom there that normally teleports you to the Queen of Hearts' castle. Walk past it without interacting and start down the ramp. As soon as you round the first corner of the ramp , look upward and you'll see the Motif positioned behind a tree overhead.

warning Don't interact with the pink mushroom by accident. Touching it sends you to the Queen's castle section, which is outside the area where the Motifs are located. Motif 4: On top of the tree portal Continue down the same ramp past where you found Motif 3. At the very bottom, you'll reach a grey and blue portal . The fourth and final Motif sits right on top of it. Working through Motifs 3 and 4 back-to-back is the most efficient approach since they're on the same path.

Once you collect all four Tweedle Dee and Tweedle Dum Motifs , leave the Realm and return to Alice to complete the quest and claim your reward. This wraps up her specific contribution to the Picture Pursuit event.

The Alice quest isn't the only Motif hunt in the event. If you're also working through the Picture Pursuit quest walkthroughs , you'll find similar hidden-object tasks tied to other characters across the valley.

tip All four Motifs are in the first forest section of the Realm only. You don't need to explore the Queen of Hearts' castle area at all for this quest. For players still tracking down hidden objects elsewhere in the event, the Walrus Motif locations guide covers the Dream Castle search for Merlin's Picture Pursuit quest. More walkthroughs, tips, and event breakdowns are available in the full Disney Dreamlight Valley guide collection .

items.title: Picture Pursuit Event Guide
items.summary: From July 15 to July 28, you'll have the chance to take part in an all-new Dreamlight Valley Event - Picture Pursuit. This event sees various Villagers,
items.content:
From July 15 to July 28 , you'll have the chance to take part in an all-new Dreamlight Valley Event - Picture Pursuit . This event sees various Villagers , such as Cinderella , Peter Pan , and Alice , sending you on scavenger hunts for motifs that have been hidden carefully around the Valley.

This event guide details everything you need to know about Picture Pursuit, including where to find all hidden motif locations, and the rewards you'll receive for completing each quest and duty.

To experience the Picture Pursuit Event in full and receive all rewards, you must first ensure you have unlocked Merlin , Mickey Mouse , Moana , Buzz , Alice , Peter Pan , and Cinderella . If you have not unlocked these characters, you won't be able to participate in their event quests.

Each day during the Picture Pursuit Event, you'll receive one brand-new quest from either Merlin , Mickey , Moana , Buzz , Alice , Peter Pan , or Cinderella . Only one of these limited quests will become available each day, so if you plan on unlocking all rewards from the event, you will need to play at least seven days in order to activate the quests.

Each limited quests will see a Villager sending you on scavenger hunts for four hidden motifs that have been scattered across the Valley. You'll need to use your Royal Phone to capture photographs of each of these motifs. Once you have captured photographs of all four motifs, return to the quest giver and you'll a motif as a reward. A list of all known rewards can be found below.

Need help finding the location of a particular motif? Click the links below to jump to our dedicated event quest guides:

Merlin has arranged a special surprise, but in order to unlock it, you'll need to find and capture photographs of four walrus motifs that he has hidden around the Dream Castle. Once you've photographed of all four hidden motifs, return to Merlin to receive a Motif Bag containing the Walrus Motif.

Here's where you can find all four Walrus motifs that are hidden around the Dream Castle.

When you enter the Dream Castle through the main entrance, you will find a walrus motif on the base of the left pillar.

When you enter the Dream Castle through the main entrance, turn right and walk between the plants found to the right of the staircase. Here, you'll find a walrus motif on the wall, next to the secret gem door.

On the third floor of the Dream Castle, a walrus motif can be found on the wall directly above the door that leads to the Expansion Realms portals.

On the fourth floor of the Dream Castle, head over to the staircase closest to the Lady and the Tramp Realm. Here, you'll find a walrus motif on the base of the staircase pillar that's closest to the windows.

Speak with Alice and she'll let you know that she has a challenge for us. To complete this challenge, we must find and capture photographs of four Tweedle Dee and Tweedle Dum motifs that have been hidden in the forest of the Alice in Wonderland Realm. Once you photographed all four hidden motifs, return to Alice to receive a Motif Bag containing the Tweedle Dee and Tweedle Dum Motif.

Here's where you can find all four Tweedle and Tweedle Dum motifs that are hidden around the Alice in Wonderland Realm.

items.title: Disney Dreamlight Valley Guide: All Walrus Motif Locations
items.summary: Find all four Walrus Motifs in Disney Dreamlight Valley's Dream Castle. Complete the Picture Pursuit Merlin quest with this floor-by-floor walkthrough.
items.content:
The Picture Pursuit event in Disney Dreamlight Valley kicks off with one of the trickier scavenger hunts in recent memory. Merlin sends you into the Dream Castle to photograph four Walrus Motifs, and the castle's multiple floors make it easy to run right past them without realizing it. This guide covers every motif location in order, so you can knock this one out without backtracking.

Before hunting anything down, you need to talk to Merlin to officially start the quest. He'll send you to the Dream Castle , located at the center of the Plaza biome . Once inside, your job is to find all four Walrus Motifs scattered across the castle's floors and photograph each one using your Phone . After snapping all four, head back to Merlin to finish the quest.

The process sounds simple, but the castle spans multiple floors and the motifs are small enough to blend into the walls and pillars. Knowing exactly where to look saves a lot of aimless wandering.

tip Equip your Phone before entering the Dream Castle so you can snap each Walrus Motif the moment you spot it without fumbling through your inventory. Where are all four Walrus Motif locations? All four motifs are spread across different floors of the Dream Castle, from the ground level entrance all the way up to the top floor. Here's the breakdown floor by floor.

Disney Dreamlight Valley Guide: All Walrus Motif Locations

This one is the easiest to grab. Step through the Dream Castle's main entrance and immediately look to your left . The first Walrus Motif is painted directly on a pillar right next to the entrance on the first floor . You'll see it before you've taken more than a few steps inside.

Stay on the first floor for this one. From the entrance, turn right and pass through the small gap between the potted hedges . Look for the door decorated with colorful gems arranged in a flower pattern. That's Mickey's secret door , and the second Walrus Motif is painted on the wall to the right of it. Easy to miss if you don't know the gem door is your landmark.

warning The potted hedges can obstruct your view as you approach. Walk all the way through the gap before looking right, or you'll think there's nothing there. Third Walrus Motif: above the Stardust Port doorway, third floor Head to the left staircase from the entrance and climb up to the second floor , where you'll pass the entrances to the Monsters, Inc. , Mulan , and Beauty and the Beast Realms. Keep going up the next set of stairs to reach the third floor . As soon as you arrive, look left and tilt your camera upward . The third Walrus Motif sits directly above the doorway leading to Stardust Port . The camera tilt is the key detail here since it's easy to scan the walls at eye level and miss it entirely.

Take the right staircase from the entrance and climb all the way to the top floor , which is the level housing the entrances to the Lady and the Tramp , Inside Out , Alice in Wonderland , Aladdin , and Hercules Realms. Reach the top of the staircase and immediately look down to your right . The fourth Walrus Motif is on a pillar at that level. After testing the route a few times, going right from the start is the fastest path to this one.

tip You don't need to find the Walrus Motifs in any specific order. Grab them in whatever sequence makes sense based on which staircase you take first. What comes after the Merlin quest? Finishing the Picture Pursuit: Merlin quest is just the start of the larger Picture Pursuit event. The valley has plenty more scavenger hunt content to work through, and other villagers have their own missions waiting. If you're working through the full event, the Disney Dreamlight Valley guides hub has walkthroughs for other quests running alongside it.

For players tackling other active storylines between Picture Pursuit missions, the Aquatic Musings quest walkthrough covers finding the Grayscale Trout and stopping Ursula's scheme, while the A Tangled Web quest guide walks you through uncovering the Realmweaver mystery. Both are solid next steps if you want to keep the quest log moving while the Picture Pursuit event runs.

items.title: Get Ready for the Picture Pursuit Event!
items.content:
Back To News Get Ready for the Picture Pursuit Event! Events 15 Jul 2026 3:00 PM Hey Valley Villager,

Break out your Royal Phone and get ready for a photo-filled adventure as the Picture Pursuit event has begun!

From July 15 to July 28, various Villagers will send you on exciting scavenger hunts across the Valley to track hidden motifs. Explore familiar places, snap the right shots, and uncover exclusive rewards along the way.

During Picture Pursuit, select Villagers will offer you limited-time quests where you'll search for specific motifs hidden throughout different areas of the Valley.

One Villager quest is available per day, so be sure to check in daily to see who needs your help!

To participate fully, make sure you've unlocked the following Villagers: Merlin, Mickey Mouse, Moana, Buzz Lightyear, Alice, Peter Pan, and Cinderella.

Only unlocked Villagers can offer their Picture Pursuit quests.

Complete quests to unlock a growing collection of themed motifs inspired by iconic Disney and Pixar worlds and characters from Merlin, Mickey & Friends, Moana, Toy Story, Alice in Wonderland, Peter Pan, and Cinderella.

Have fun exploring, and we can't wait to see your shots! ____________________________________________________

Be sure to follow our social channels for more news and updates!

items.title: Honeyglow Woods Expansion Guide
items.summary: Welcome to Honeyglow Woods, a brand-new Disney Dreamlight Valley expansion that's inspired by the iconic Hundred Acre Wood. This new land is packed with
items.content:
Welcome to Honeyglow Woods , a brand-new Disney Dreamlight Valley expansion that's inspired by the iconic Hundred Acre Wood . This new land is packed with puzzles , secrets , and tons of magical Golden Honey. As you explore the area, you'll stumble upon Winnie the Pooh , Piglet , and Eeyore , who you'll be tasked to reunite and welcome back to the Valley.

To ensure you don't miss a single Friendship Quest , new Recipe , Crafting Material , Hedgehog Critter , or mini-game, we've got you covered with our comprehensive Honeyglow Woods Expansion Guide below.

Looking for details on a particular Recipe, Quest, or Resource? Click the links below to jump to:

Want to speed up Golden Honey production? See our guide on How to Get More Busy Bees Houses to learn more.

Available immediately at launch, you'll have full access to three brand-new characters, Winnie the Pooh , Piglet , and Eeyore , each featuring their own unique Story and Friendship Quests to complete. To learn more about each new character , including their Quests, click the links below:

Winnie the Pooh makes his grand debut in Dreamlight Valley as part of the Honeyglow Woods expansion. You'll find Pooh in the Drowsybloom Acre , where he'll need your assistance to restore his home, track down some misplaced honey, and reunite him with his missing pals.

Eeyore is encountered in the Gloommeadow biome of Honeyglow Woods. This area is initially blocked by a Corrupted Honey Wall, but you unlock the pathway during the Quest "Interlude: The Misplaced Honey." In the Gloommeadow, you can help Eeyore restore a dried river, face down a monster, repair his House, and more!

During "Interlude 2: The Gloomy Hollow," you clear out the second of the Corrupted Honey Walls, which unlocks the path to Braveheart Grove. There, you can meet Piglet, who has holed himself up in his House! Help Piglet conquer his fears to unlock him as a Villager!

There are four sub-biomes in Honeyglow Woods, each with a unique Hedgehog critters for you to befriend and unlock as companions . The four Hedgehog variants include the Classic Hedgehog , the Green Hedgehog , the Yellow Hedgehog , and the Orange Hedgehog .

For full details on how to approach Hedgehogs, as well as their favorite foods, see our comprehensive guides below to learn everything you need to know.

There are 12 brand-new recipes that are exclusive to Honeyglow Woods. With recipes that range from one-star classics to intricate five-star meals , we recommend keeping our Honeyglow Woods Recipe Guide handy, as it provides a comprehensive look at all the ingredients needed to make every new recipe.

In addition to new recipes and critters, you'll also find several new ingredients and fish to uncover in Honeyglow Woods. To learn more about obtaining these new items, see our handy guides below for all the details.

There are eight new flowers to collect, two new gem variants to mine, and three new crafting materials to find. To learn more about obtaining these items, as well as using our checklist tool to keep track of them, see our guides below:

items.title: How to Unlock Winnie the Pooh
items.summary: Winnie the Pooh is the first character you will meet in Disney Dreamlight Valley's Honeyglow Woods expansion. You can encounter him in the Drowsybloom
items.content:
Winnie the Pooh is the first character you will meet in Disney Dreamlight Valley's Honeyglow Woods expansion . You can encounter him in the Drowsybloom Acre biome as soon as you travel from the Valley into the Honeyglow Woods. However, he does not immediately unlock as a Villager - you have to complete several Quests before he will officially do so.

This guide covers all Quests you need to complete to unlock Winnie the Pooh in Disney Dreamlight Valley!

In this brief Prologue quest, you need to open your mailbox to find a Treasure Chest inside. In the box is a Tree Sprout that, when placed anywhere in your Valley, will turn into a tree portal. Head through the portal to enter the Honeyglow Woods!

Meet Winnie the Pooh in the Honeyglow Woods in this Quest. Play a game of Hide and Seek together, then help him restore the Busy Bees' House so they can once again begin producing their delicious Golden Honey, which is used to produce all of Pooh's favorite snacks.

Crafta Honeyglow Elixir so that you can clear the fog around Drowsybloom Acre to reunite Pooh with his Thoughtful Spot. Then, gather lots of materials in Drowsybloom Acre to restore the Thoughtful Spot to its former glory!

Splash through some puddles with Pooh on a rainy day in the Honeyglow Woods! Then, help him restore his House so that he has somewhere comfortable to live.

At the end of "A Very Satisfied Bear," Pooh will join the Valley as a Villager. You can raise his Friendship Level by Hanging Out with him, having Daily Discussions, and giving him his favorite Gifts - which, predictably, tend to be recipes that involve Golden Honey!

items.title: How to Unlock Piglet
items.summary: Piglet is the third character you will unlock in Disney Dreamlight Valley's Honeyglow Woods expansion. This shy little guy has holed himself up in his
items.content:
Piglet is the third character you will unlock in Disney Dreamlight Valley's Honeyglow Woods expansion . This shy little guy has holed himself up in his house in the Braveheart Grove biome, and you will have to complete a good number of Quests before he will officially join your Valley. Fortunately, this guide will tell you exactly what you need to do to unlock Piglet as a Villager, so that you can raise his Friendship Level and claim his fun Friendship Rewards!

Before meeting Piglet, you need to encounter and unlock two other characters in the Honeyglow Woods. The first of these is Winnie the Pooh, who lives in the Drowsybloom Acre biome. You will have to help him restore the Busy Bees' House, his Thoughtful Spot, and his House, all key locations within Drowsybloom Acre.

See our detailed guide to unlocking Winnie the Pooh as well as walkthroughs for all Quests required:

Pooh joins as a Villager at the end of "Chapter 3: A Very Satisfied Bear," after you free his House from the encroaching fog.

After unlocking Pooh, you will journey with him inside the Everoak Tree to open the path to the Gloommeadow, the second biome of Honeyglow Woods. There, you will meet Eeyore, and will have to complete several tasks before he will join the Valley. These include helping Eeyore find his tail, protecting him from the Big Red Monster, and restoring his House, which fell over.

Check out our detailed guide to unlocking Eeyore as well as our walkthroughs for each Quest you will need to complete:

Eeyore will join the Valley at the end of "Chapter 6: A Place For Eeyore," after you fix up his House and add decorations to it.

With Eeyore moved into the Valley, you can enter the Everoak once more and bring color back to the Gloomy Hollow. This lets you clear the Corrupted Honey Wall leading to Braveheart Grove, granting you access to the third biome of the Honeyglow Woods.

In Braveheart Grove, you will meet Piglet, but he is too afraid to leave his House. Cook him tasty food and take pictures of the area to convince Piglet that it is safe to explore outside once more!

Piglet is sad because the sounds that he loves are missing from Braveheart Grove. Craft some noise-making furniture for him, and restore the Babbling Pond to bring sound back to the Biome!

Piglet realizes that you need to enter the Everoak Tree to restore Braveheart Grove, but the paints are missing! Work with Pooh, Piglet and Eeyore to find the three missing Watercolors you will need to complete the painting outside the Everoak. Along the way, you will restore the bridge connecting Drowsybloom Acre with Braveheart Grove so that Pooh and Piglet can visit with each other!

At the end of Chapter 9, Piglet officially joins the Valley as a Villager. You can Hang Out with him, raise his Friendship Level, and complete his Friendship Quests to earn all sorts of rewards!

items.title: Disney Dreamlight Valley Guide: All Hidden Green Chest Locations
items.summary: Find all 9 hidden green chests in Honeyglow Woods and claim exclusive rewards. Full locations, required tools, and unlock tips in one guide.
items.content:
Disney Dreamlight Valley packs a lot of secrets into the Honeyglow Woods region, and the nine hidden green chests scattered across its biomes are among the best finds. Each one holds a reward you can't get anywhere else, from cosmetic clothing pieces to a glider, so skipping them means permanently missing out on exclusive items. The catch is that several chests require specific Royal Tool upgrades and story progress before you can actually open them.

Before going chest hunting, it's worth knowing that Honeyglow Woods splits across multiple biomes and four sections of the Everoak Tree. You'll need access to all of them plus a couple of key upgrades to clean out every chest. The safest approach is to push through the main storyline first, then sweep the area once you have everything unlocked.

Most of the outdoor chests are embedded in rocky walls and need Driftwood to free them. One piece per chest is all it takes. The chests inside the Everoak Tree are a different story. Several are blocked by Tree Resin log spots , which require the Amber Brushwoods Pickaxe Upgrade to break through. You unlock this upgrade during the Chapter 6: A Place for Eeyore quest. One chest in the fourth Everoak Tree section is only blocked by Honey Swirls , which you can clear with magic, making it the most accessible of the tree chests.

A second tree chest, found in the second Everoak section, is blocked by obstacles that need the Prickle-Popper Potion applied to your Royal Shovel. You craft this during the Chapter 8: The Little Big Forest quest. Plan your chest run around these two quest milestones and you'll avoid backtracking.

The first chest sits in the upper right corner of Drowsybloom Acre , tucked into the wall directly behind where your house starts in this region. Open it with Driftwood to claim the Honeyglow Glider . The third chest is in the upper left corner of the same biome, close to the bridge leading up toward Braveheart Grove. One piece of Driftwood frees it, and it contains the Blooming Knitted Cardigan .

If you're still early in the expansion and haven't started farming yet, the Hunny Seed Stall unlock guide covers how to get set up in Drowsybloom Acre while you're in the area.

Head to the very north end of Gloommeadow and look for the ramp leading up to Braveheart Grove. The fourth chest is in the wall right beside that ramp. Use Driftwood to open it and you'll walk away with the Sunny Picnic Dress .

The sixth chest is in the upper left corner of Braveheart Grove , embedded in the rocky wall. Driftwood is all you need here, and the reward is the Striped Busy Bees' Socks .

This one is genuinely easy to miss. Chest 8 is almost completely buried in the rock wall near the east end of Nectar Apiary . If you're struggling to spot it, remove any trees you've placed near the marked wall area to clear your line of sight. Driftwood opens it, and it holds the Cozy Acorn Beret .

warning Chest 8 in Nectar Apiary is the hardest to see in the entire region. If you've placed decorative trees near the east wall, they can completely obscure it. Clear the area before assuming the chest isn't there. Everoak Tree sections (chests 2, 5, 7, and 9) Four chests are spread across the four sections of the Everoak Tree , each accessed from a different biome during specific quests.

Chest 2 is in the first Everoak Tree section, entered from Drowsybloom Acre during the Interlude: The Misplaced Honey quest . It's in a small alcove on your right as you enter, but Tree Resin logs block the way until you have the Amber Brushwoods Pickaxe Upgrade from Chapter 6. The reward is a Yellow Raincoat .

Chest 5 is in the second Everoak Tree section, accessed from Gloommeadow during the Interlude 2: The Gloomy Hollow quest. It's behind Tree Resin logs on the right side of Eeyore's puzzle. You need the Prickle-Popper Potion on your Royal Shovel, unlocked in Chapter 8, to clear the path. The reward is the Summer Blossom Braids hairstyle.

Chest 7 is in the third Everoak Tree section, entered from Braveheart Grove during the Interlude 3: The Shadowy Surprise quest. It's tucked in a corner behind the central puzzle. The Amber Brushwoods Pickaxe Upgrade handles the Tree Resin logs here, and the reward is the Honeybee Gold Earrings .

Chest 9 is in the fourth and final Everoak Tree section, accessed from Nectar Apiary during the Interlude 4: Curiosity Satisfied quest. Find it on the left side of the stairs leading to the cooking station where you prepare the Hedgehog Treat. Only Honey Swirls block it, cleared with magic, making this the easiest Everoak chest. The reward is the Honeybee Gold Necklace .

items.title: Disney Dreamlight Valley Grown-Up Things Quest Walkthrough
items.summary: Complete the Grown-Up Things quest in Disney Dreamlight Valley. Find all toys, craft a Honeyglow Flower Pot, and unlock the mystery chest with Pooh.
items.content:
Winnie the Pooh's first friendship quest in Disney Dreamlight Valley is called Grown-Up Things , and it sends you on a surprisingly involved tour of Drowsybloom Acre. Pooh wants to understand what being a grown-up actually means, which translates into a cleaning session, a decorating project, and a miniature treasure hunt all rolled into one. The quest has enough moving parts that it's easy to miss a step, so here's the full breakdown.

After unlocking Winnie the Pooh and placing him in your Valley, speak to him to trigger Grown-Up Things . This is the first quest in his friendship arc, so you won't be able to skip ahead to later missions without completing it first.

The first thing Pooh asks is to see what a grown-up looks like, so you need to dress the part. Your outfit must include all three of the following at the same time:

Open your wardrobe, filter by these tags, and mix and match until all three boxes are checked. Once your outfit qualifies, head back to Pooh and show him your grown-up look to move on.

tip You don't need three separate pieces for each category. A single clothing item can satisfy more than one tag simultaneously, so check each piece's tags carefully before assuming you need to swap everything out. How to find the toys in Drowsybloom Acre With the outfit sorted, Pooh decides that cleaning is a very grown-up activity. Your next task is to clear corrupted Honey Swirls scattered around Drowsybloom Acre to uncover hidden toys. You're looking for two specific items: a Toy Car and some Beach Toys .

Not every Honey Swirl in the area contains a toy. The two you need look visually different from the others because you can see the item inside the swirl itself. Scout the area and target the swirls that have something visible inside them rather than clearing every single one.

The third toy, the Toy Butterfly , doesn't sit still. It flies around Drowsybloom Acre and you have to chase it down. If you're finding it difficult to close the gap, cooking and eating Honeyglow Woods meals can grant you the gliding ability , which significantly increases your movement speed and makes the chase much more manageable.

Once the area is cleaner, you need to craft a Honeyglow Flower Pot to decorate with. Any of the four variants counts, so pick whichever one you have the materials for.

Every recipe costs one Golden Honey , so make sure you have at least one in your inventory before heading to the crafting table. The Thistle and Foxglove variants differ by color, so grab whichever matching pair you already have on hand.

With the flower pot crafted, it's time to actually decorate. The quest requires you to place all of the following in Drowsybloom Acre:

Placement doesn't need to be permanent or aesthetically perfect. You can remove everything after the quest is complete, so just get the items down anywhere in the area. After placing them all, talk to Winnie the Pooh to check in on your progress.

tip If you're short on fences or paths, check your furniture storage first. Many players accumulate these through earlier quests and rewards without realizing it. How to find the toy inside Pooh's house The clean-up continues indoors. Head inside Winnie the Pooh's house and look for another corrupted Honey Swirl . Clear it to collect one more toy. After that, you'll have gathered everything you need to restore Pooh's shelves.

With all the toys collected, interact with the four sparkling spots inside Pooh's house to return each toy to its proper place on the shelves. These spots are spread around the interior, so check every corner until all four have been activated.

While returning the toys, you'll notice something wedged under the bed. Talk to Pooh about it, then approach the sparkling spot on the right side of the bed. Use a piece of Driftwood to pry the object loose. A locked green chest will appear nearby.

items.title: Discover the Honeyglow Woods Adventure Pack!
items.summary: Hey Valley Villagers, A brand-new cozy chapter begins today in Disney Dreamlight Valley as you step through a magical gateway into the Honeyglow Woods, a forgotten land where childhood memories, beloved friends, and the gentle magic of hone
items.content:
Hey Valley Villagers,

A brand-new cozy chapter begins today in Disney Dreamlight Valley as you step through a magical gateway into the Honeyglow Woods, a forgotten land where childhood memories, beloved friends, and the gentle magic of honey lingers just beyond the fog.

A Mysterious Invitation

Your journey begins with a mysterious package left in your mailbox, containing treasured childhood keepsakes and a curious little Tree Sprout. Once planted, the Tree Sprout grows into a magical gateway to the Honeyglow Woods itself.

But something isn't quite right.

A strange fog has settled across the Woods, and its once carefree residents have become separated from one another. To start setting things right, you'll meet Winnie the Pooh and work alongside him to uncover clues, clear the fog, and restore the warmth that once filled the Woods.

New Places & Beloved Friends

As you journey deeper into the Honeyglow Woods, you'll discover enchanting new places inspired by the world of Winnie the Pooh, each filled with its own atmosphere, secrets, and stories to uncover.

Wander through the flower-filled meadows of Drowsybloom Acre, splash through misty marshes in the Gloommeadow or brave the deeper woods hidden beyond the fog to eventually uncover the hidden Nectar Apiary, where the source of the Woods' troubles quietly waits.

Along the way, you'll share heartfelt moments with Winnie the Pooh, Eeyore, and Piglet. Play games of hide-and-seek with Pooh, help Eeyore search for his missing tail, and encourage Piglet to find courage in the face of fear, all while restoring the warmth that once filled this magical place.

 

Cozy Adventures Beneath the Trees

Beyond the mist, the Honeyglow Woods are full of gentle surprises and cozy discoveries. Care for adorable hedgehogs, restore Busy Bees' Houses to unlock beekeeping adventures, and gather Golden Honey to craft recipes, furniture, and decorations inspired by the golden glow of the Woods.

Take part in playful moments like cloud-watching, rainy puddle-jumping, and Pooh Sticks along the riverbanks, where even the simplest activities become part of a shared adventure with your friends.

The Adventure Begins Today

Disney Dreamlight Valley: Honeyglow Woods is available now as a standalone adventure pack or as part of the Honeyglow Woods Edition for new players looking to begin their journey.

Players who play Honeyglow Woods before July 22nd will also receive the exclusive Rainy Day Winnie the Pooh Dream Style delivered directly to their in-game mailbox.

We can't wait for you to step through the magical glowing tree, rediscover beloved friends, and uncover the adventure waiting within the Honeyglow Woods!

items.title: Disney Dreamlight Valley announces big Honeyglow Woods Adventure Pack DLC [update: trailer]
items.summary: Disney Dreamlight Valley Honeyglow Woods Adventure Pack DLC now revealed, and here's everything that it includes.
items.content:
June 17: A summer showcase was held for Disney Dreamlight Valley in which Gameloft detailed what’s to come for the game, bed by a Honeyglow Woods Adventure Pack.

The DLC adds a new storyline with Winnie the Pooh, Piglet, and Eeyore from Disney’s Winnie the Pooh. That’s along with Honeyglow Woods, beekeeping and Pooh Sticks, new items, and more.

This Adventure Pack introduces new experiences taking place in the Honeyglow Woods to be enjoyed alongside beloved new characters from Disney’s Winnie the Pooh. This comes with a heartfelt new story, a new Biome and more features that blend nostalgia, discovery, and unforgettable adventures.

Here’s a quick summary of what was presented in the Summer Showcase video:

Along with the above, new editions are launching on July 8, 2026. These are as follows:

The full recording of today’s showcase can be found below.

Disney Dreamlight Valley is out on Nintendo Switch 2 and Switch. Information about the game’s previous updates can be found here.

July 8: A trailer has now been released, which we have below.

items.title: Honeyglow Woods Update Patch Notes
items.summary: Hey Valley Villagers! This week, we celebrate 100 years of Winnie the Pooh with our new adventure pack Disney Dreamlight Valley: Honeyglow Woods. We invite you to cross through a magical new gateway into a magnificent place, inspired by the
items.content:
Hey Valley Villagers!

This week, we celebrate 100 years of Winnie the Pooh with our new adventure pack Disney Dreamlight Valley: Honeyglow Woods. We invite you to cross through a magical new gateway into a magnificent place, inspired by the Hundred Acre Wood, made up of four unique sub-biomes filled with mystery and magical honey!

Honeyglow Woods is the first ever Disney Dreamlight Valley adventure pack – a new optional paid add-on to be experienced alongside our usual free content updates. This is a new type of experience that allows us to create a unique adventure with thematic landscapes to decorate, a brand-new story, and a cast of loveable characters for those who wish to bring a little extra Disney magic to their Village. For more on this new type of experience, check out our adventure pack FAQ:

https://disneydreamlightvalley.com/news/faq_adventurepacks

Honeyglow Woods arrives on July 8th, 2026. Check out the details below.

NEW CONTENT (HONEYGLOW WOODS ADVENTURE PACK OWNERS):

  • Embark on this new adventure by planting a Tree Sprout until it grows into a magical glowing tree gateway that you’ll be able to place anywhere in your Valley or your base game Floating Islands – you’ll also be able to remove and/or replace this magical gateway at any time.

    [/*]

  • You’ll also have special new portals in Honeyglow Woods that will take you right to Scrooge McDuck’s Store and Chez Remy’s restaurant.

    [/*]

  • Once you’ve got your gateway placed cross over to the other side, but beware, something has gone very wrong in this place...  but don’t worry, you won’t be alone on the journey!

    [/*]

  • Reunite with Pooh Bear himself before finding Eeyore, and then finally Piglet!

    [/*]

  • Invite Winnie the Pooh, Eeyore, and Piglet back to your Valley! They’ll of course arrive with their own homes, Friendship Quests and Rewards.

    [/*]

  • Discover all four new areas and solve the mystery of Honeyglow Woods: the Drowsybloom Acres, the Gloommeadow, Braveheart Grove, and the Nectar Apiary.

    [/*]

  • Make use of the abundance of honey for new honey-based recipes and furniture, like Warm Milk & Honey, Hunnycrunch Bar, Poohs Birthday Cake, Honeypot Lamp, Cozy Honeyleaf Rug, and so much more! 

    [/*]

  • Discover a brand-new gameplay mechanic, Beekeeping! Place flowers around a Busy Bees’ House, to pollinate the flowers and generate more honey in the houses – a fully pollinated Busy Bees’ House will increase the growth speeds of other nearby flowers!

    [/*]

  • You'll also be able to move these Busy Bees’ Houses to any of your other biomes, including your base game Valley!

    [/*]

  • Keep your eyes peeled for the absolutely adorable Hedgehogs! These new little cuties will come with their own Photo Mode poses!

    [/*]

  • Try interacting with a Honeyglow bridge in the Gloommeadow to play Pooh Sticks! A new and unique way to interact and level up your Friendship Level with your villagers and unlock their exclusive rewards!

    [/*]

  • For those of you already playing Disney Dreamlight Valley, you'll be able to enjoy Honeyglow Woods on all platforms. What's more, you'll get 2,000 Moonstones to spend however you please!

    [/*]

  • If you're brand new to Disney Dreamlight Valley, you can get Honeyglow Woods AND the base game with 10,000 Moonstones! (Psst, that's a special currency you can spend on exclusive furniture and clothing in our Premium Shop.) 

    [/*]

  • Finaly, if you embark on this new adventure before July 22nd, you’ll also unlock an exclusive Rainy Day Winnie the Pooh Dream Style in your in-game mailbox with your purchase!

    [/*]

Disney Dreamlight Valley Arcade Edition owners will have access to the contents of the Honeyglow Woods adventure pack on day one without the need for additional purchase.

LAUNCH DAY TWITCH DROPS (AVAILABLE TO ALL PLAYERS)

For the release of this exciting new adventure pack, we’re launching a new Twitch Drops activation!

From July 8th at 10:00 AM EDT to July 15th at 9:59 AM EDT, you can head over to the following partnered creators and watch their Disney Dreamlight Valley streams to unlock Piglet’s Clothesline!

For participation and partnered creators’ details, visit our website:

https://disneydreamlightvalley.com/twitch-drops

NEW CONTENT AND IMPROVEMENTS (AVAILABLE TO ALL PLAYERS):

Revamped Fast Travel Layout

  • A new interactive menu for Fast Travel that will provide more clarity and context to the whole feature and will let you “favorite” the locations you most want to visit!

    [/*]

TOP CHANGES AND BUG FIXES (AVAILABLE TO ALL PLAYERS):

  • Fixed an issue where the Raindrops visual was not displayed on several platforms.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where some assets visuals would not appear such as the Highland Will-O’-Wisps, Dragonflies, Autumnal Clock Tower books, The Butterfly Tiara and Enchanted Shooting Stars.

    [/*]

  • A brush icon was added to the Basic Toga Dress to identify it as a Touch of Magic item.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue on the Apple Arcade platform where motifs couldn't be placed in Photo Mode.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where Daisy Design Challenges could be completed with Decoration Preset mirages.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where socks would have a broken texture when wearing the Midnight Sun Slippers.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where the Medium and Large Painted Cottage chests were missing from the Scrooge Delivery.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue with group mode where Undo is applied to items one by one when a group of items has been moved to another Biome.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where the Avatar's face is distorted in the Reward Screen when equipping certain umbrellas.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue in the Equine Delicacies quest where the player may not receive the Stainless Steel Pot Lid.

    [/*]

  • There is a known issue with the “Interlude 2: The Gloomy Hollow” quest wherein Eeyore might get stuck. This issue can be easily bypassed by removing at least 2 obstacles from his path.

    • This issue will be fully addressed in our next update.

      [/*]
    [/*]

items.summary: Disney Dreamlight Valley its releasing its Honeyglow Woods update this week, and we now have patch notes to go along with it. This goes live on July 8, 2026. Honeyglow Woods is primarily DLC. However, Gameloft is bringing about an update which will also include a few things for all players. As part of this, everyone will be able to...
items.content:
Disney Dreamlight Valley its releasing its Honeyglow Woods update this week, and we now have patch notes to go along with it. This goes live on July 8, 2026.

Honeyglow Woods is primarily DLC. However, Gameloft is bringing about an update which will also include a few things for all players. As part of this, everyone will be able to access a new interactive menu for Fast Travel.

The full rundown on what’s to come is as follows:

NEW CONTENT (HONEYGLOW WOODS ADVENTURE PACK OWNERS):

Disney Dreamlight Valley Arcade Edition owners will have access to the contents of the Honeyglow Woods adventure pack on day one without the need for additional purchase.

LAUNCH DAY TWITCH DROPS (AVAILABLE TO ALL PLAYERS)

For the release of this exciting new adventure pack, we’re launching a new Twitch Drops activation! From July 8th at 10:00 AM EDT to July 15th at 9:59 AM EDT, you can head over to the following partnered creators and watch their Disney Dreamlight Valley streams to unlock Piglet’s Clothesline! For participation and partnered creators’ details, visit our website: https://disneydreamlightvalley.com/twitch-drops

NEW CONTENT AND IMPROVEMENTS (AVAILABLE TO ALL PLAYERS):

TOP CHANGES AND BUG FIXES (AVAILABLE TO ALL PLAYERS):

Disney Dreamlight Valley is available on Nintendo Switch 2 and Switch. Tons of news about the game can be found here .

items.title: Catch Up on the Disney Dreamlight Valley 2026 Summer Showcase Stream!
items.summary: Hey, Valley Villagers! Today, we had the absolute pleasure of announcing a totally unique adventure with the Disney Dreamlight Valley: Honeyglow Woods Adventure Pack, arriving on July 8, 2026! We then pulled back the curtains and peeked at
items.content:
Hey, Valley Villagers!

Today, we had the absolute pleasure of announcing a totally unique adventure with the Disney Dreamlight Valley: Honeyglow Woods Adventure Pack, arriving on July 8, 2026! We then pulled back the curtains and peeked at some of the upcoming free updates you can expect in the back half of the year.

If you missed the Summer Showcase stream, the best way to catch up is by watching it right here:

Welcome to the Honeyglow Woods

Celebrate 100 years of Winnie the Pooh on July 8 with our brand-new adventure Disney Dreamlight Valley: Honeyglow Woods!Cross through a magical new gateway into the titular Honeyglow Woods, a land inspired by Winnie the Pooh, filled with puzzles, lost secrets, and a fair share of honey. 🍯

The Honeyglow Woods were once a place where you could enjoy just doing nothing... at least until the Everoak Tree at the center of the Woods closed up.

To embark on this new adventure, you'll need to plant a Tree Sprout until it grows into a magical glowing tree that you'll be able to place anywhere in your Valley, your Floating Islands, or your Expansions—you'll also be able to remove and/or replace this magical gateway at any time. Once you've crossed over to the other side, you'll notice something has gone very wrong in this place... But don't worry, you won't be alone on the journey!

The Friends You Make Along the Way

Soon you'll reunite with Winnie the Pooh, Piglet, and Eeyore to return to the fun, carefree days of childhood!

First, you'll meet the Pooh Bear himself: Winnie the Pooh! Spend some time playing hide-and-seek and watching the clouds go by, then uncover the magic of honey—magical honey that is! Oh, and well, it looks like Eeyore has lost his tail again. Help this delightfully gloomy donkey find a new tail until you can find the real one. Lastly, help the courageous Piglet face his fears, and reunite the trio!

Before you know it, you'll be able to invite Winnie the Pooh, Eeyore, and Piglet back to your Valley! They'll of course arrive with their own homes, Friendship Quests and rewards... but first you'll want to solve the mystery of the Honeyglow Woods.

A New Magical Land, with Magical Honey

This magical place, inspired by the Hundred Acre Wood, was where you would spend splendid lazy summers as a kid. It represents a new space to decorate and make your own with four special new areas to discover and progress through.

First is the Drowsybloom Acre, a sunny meadow perfect for napping and smelling flowers. Then there's the Gloommeadow, a murky marsh full of ponds, puddles, and rivers. There's also Braveheart Grove, a dense forested area where you (and Piglet) will face your fears. Finally, there's the Nectar Apiary, a hidden glen filled with honeycombs where you'll find the source of the troubles!

To get to the Honeyglow Woods, you'll use the previously mentioned magical glowing tree. You'll also have special new portals in the Honeyglow Woods that will take you right to Scrooge McDuck's Store and Chez Remy's restaurant.

Speaking of food, in the Honeyglow Woods, you'll be able to use the honey from the bee boxes to unlock new honey-based recipes and furniture, like Warm Milk & Honey, Honeycrunch Bar, Pooh's Birthday Cake, Honeypot Lamp, Cozy Honeyleaf Rug, and so much more!

But wait, that's not the only way you'll be able to use honey! This Adventure Pack also contains a brand-new gameplay mechanic: beekeeping! By placing flowers around a Busy Bees' House, they'll get pollinated, which will generate more honey in the houses. In addition to this, a fully pollinated Busy Bees' House will double the growth of flowers in the same Biome up to their spawn limit. You'll also be able to move these Busy Bees' Houses to any of your other Biomes, including your base game Valley! 

And of course, no new lands in Disney Dreamlight Valley would be complete without a new kind of critter... behold the absolutely adorable hedgehogs! These little cuties will come with their own Photo Mode poses and Friendship Levels to unlock!

The Honeyglow Woods Adventure Pack also introduces a new mini-game: Pooh Sticks! By interacting with a Honeyglow bridge in the Gloommeadow, you'll be able to aim and drop a stick (and other items) to race them down the river. This is a new and unique way to interact and level up your Friendship Level with your Villagers and unlock exclusive rewards!

The Adventure Pack and Early Adopter Bonus

The Honeyglow Woods is the first ever Disney Dreamlight Valley Adventure Pack—an optional, paid new add-on to be experienced alongside our usual free content updates. Diving into this sort of adventure allows us to create a unique experience inspired by one beloved universe with thematic landscapes to decorate, a brand-new story, and a cast of lovable Characters for those who wish to bring a little extra Disney magic to their Village. For more on this new type of experience, check out our Adventure Pack FAQ: https://disneydreamlightvalley.com/news/faq_adventurepacks

For those of you already playing Disney Dreamlight Valley, you'll be able to enjoy the Honeyglow Woods on all platforms or purchase this new adventure in the Premium Shop. What's more, you'll get 2,000 Moonstones to spend however you please!

If you're brand-new to Disney Dreamlight Valley, you can get the Honeyglow Woods AND the base game with 10,000 Moonstones! (Psst, that's a special currency you can spend on exclusive furniture and clothing in our Premium Shop.) 

For anyone playing either the standalone Adventure Pack or the bundled edition with the base game before July 22, you'll also receive an exclusive Rainy Day Winnie the Pooh Dream Style in your in-game mailbox with your purchase!

Disney Dreamlight Valley Apple Arcade Edition owners will have access to the contents of the Honeyglow Woods Adventure Pack on day one without the need for an additional purchase.

What's Next?

We're already halfway through the year, but we're just getting started! Later this summer, you'll meet a long-awaited Villager: Ralph, from Disney's Wreck-It Ralph, who will finally be joining Vanellope in the Valley!

Ralph will arrive in the Valley with some help from the Forgotten. Get swept into video game nostalgia as you help Ralph run a Bad-Anon meeting, reconnect with Vanellope, and more! This update will also include a new Star Path, features, and quality-of-life improvements—we'll have more info coming soon!

Then later in the year, you can expect two more free base game updates, and a new Disney Dreamlight Valley Expansion... But we'll dive into that at a later date. 😉

Community House Style

We're thrilled to announce a new community activation where you can have your say on what kind of House we should be adding to the Valley later this year!

Use the link below to start voting—all rounds are open until June 26!

https://disneydreamlightvalley.com/community-house-voting

Twitch Drops

Last but not least, this Summer Showcase also kicked off a new Twitch Drops activation!

From today, June 17, until June 24,you can watch our partnered creators' Disney Dreamlight Valley streams to unlock an Eeyore Ears Headband!

For participation and partnered creators' details, visit our website:

disneydreamlightvalley.com/twitch-drops

_________________________________________________________

We can't wait for you to experience everything in this new Adventure Pack when Disney Dreamlight Valley: Honeyglow Woods launches on July 8, and for you to welcome Ralph to your Valleys in our free content update. Thank you for taking part in this magical journey with us!

See you in the Valley, and the Honeyglow Woods!

items.title: Discover A Hero's Journey Update
items.summary: Hey Valley Villagers, Prepare for an epic new adventure with our latest update: A Hero’s Journey, now available on all platforms! Step into a brand-new Realm and embark on a series of heroic trials to uncover the mystery behind the restless
items.content:
Hey Valley Villagers,

Prepare for an epic new adventure with our latest update: A Hero’s Journey, now available on all platforms! 

Step into a brand-new Realm and embark on a series of heroic trials to uncover the mystery behind the restless gods and help guide Hercules from Disney’s Hercules back to your Valley, working alongside his legendary mentor Phil from Disney’s Hercules.

A Realm Worthy of a Hero

To begin your journey, unlock the Hercules Realm and explore Phil’s training island.  The moment you arrive, it’s easy to see that something’s not quite right. Dark Thorns have started to appear, and a mysterious fog has settled over the woods.

With Hercules nowhere to be found after heading deeper into the forest, it’s up to you to follow the trail and discover what’s happened.

Train like a Hero

To make sure you’re strong enough to chase Hercules into the fog, Phil has prepared special training challenges. Take on obstacle courses where you’ll clear Dark Thorns, activate Moonlight Beacons, and collect rewards as you prove your skills.

As you progress, unlock new abilities and divine tools that will help you on your journey. Travel through shadowed forests, across perilous cliffs, and into an ancient temple. Along the way, earn the favor of different gods through troubled terrain, and ultimately solve the mystery of who caused the chaos in the Realm in the first place.  When you’re done, you’ll guide Hercules and Phil back to the Valley.

Godly Glamor Star Path

Channel your inner deity with the Godly Glamor Star Path, where myths meet elegance.

Earn radiant new outfits, divine-inspired furniture, and mythical companions as you progress through your journey. From glowing celestial designs to majestic decor fit for the gods, every reward will bring a touch of Olympus to your Valley.

Also unlock standout rewards such as the Mythical Cloud Glider, Pink Baby Pegasus companion, Hercules’ Divine Glow Dream Style, and the Olympian Cloud Mansion House Style.

A Heroic Update to the Premium Shop

The Premium Shop is getting a heroic refresh with new items inspired by the world of Hercules and the gods.

Step into mythic style with the Gilded Agora Building Kit, featuring the majestic Grecian Villa House Style, and the ability to summon a fierce new ally with the “good-good-good” puppy Cerberus companion. 

You’ll also find the new Cozy Companion Rajah Bundle, featuring Rajah, Jasmine’s loyal companion, bringing unique interactions with Aladdin and Jasmine, Photo Mode poses, an exclusive friendship reward, and even a Tiger Cub companion. 

Additional highlights include a new Dream Style for Ariel, Moana’s parade float, and new mount accessories arriving in the Shop.

New Ways to Decorate Your Valley

We’re introducing Decoration Presets, a brand-new way to decorate your Valley with ease.

This feature works alongside Group Mode and allows you to select a section of your Valley and save it as a Preset. You can then remove, copy, and replace fully decorated areas whenever you like.

If you don’t own enough of an item in a saved Preset, a Mirage placeholder will appear, letting you quickly craft or purchase what’s missing and place it instantly.

\

Olaf’s Grand Exhibition Expands

Olaf’s Grand Exhibition is growing with two new rooms arriving in this update: the Forest of Valor and the Glade of Trust.

Continue building your collection and celebrate your Valley journey by showcasing even more of your favorite memories and achievements.

Valley Events

There are two exciting events arriving with the A Hero’s Journey Update for you to enjoy in the Valley:

Dreamlight Parks Fest (June 10 – June 30)

Celebrate the return of the Dreamlight Parks Fest! Collect buttons throughout your Valley to earn returning favorites as well as brand-new rewards.

Picture Pursuit Event (July 15 – July 28)

Embark on a new photographic adventure. Complete daily quests, find mysterious motifs, and capture them in photos to unlock exclusive rewards, including a new Photo Mode frame!

We can’t wait for you to explore all the heroic adventures A Hero’s Journey has to offer! Don’t forget to connect with us on our official social media to share your in-game adventures by tagging us in your content.   

  • Facebook: https://gmlft.co/DDV-Facebook

    [/*]

  • Instagram: https://gmlft.co/DDV-Instagram   

    [/*]

  • X: https://gmlft.co/DDV-X   

    [/*]

  • YouTube: https://gmlft.co/DDV-YouTube   

    [/*]

  • TikTok: https://gmlft.co/DDV-TikTok   

    [/*]

As always, should you run into any issues while playing Disney Dreamlight Valley, we first recommend that you check out our known issues here. If you don’t see the issue you’re running into, you can get in touch with our Support team via our contact form. Stumped by a certain quest or objective? Check out our friendly Discord Community for some Disney Dreamlight Valley tips and tricks!

items.title: A Hero’s Journey Update Patch Notes
items.summary: Hey Valley Villagers! We’re thrilled to have shared a first look at the A Hero’s Journey free update in our latest Developer Update video! Now, let’s jump into the details of what you can expect in this update. In A Hero’s Journey, you’ll t
items.content:
Hey Valley Villagers!

We’re thrilled to have shared a first look at the A Hero’s Journey free update in our latest Developer Update video! Now, let’s jump into the details of what you can expect in this update.

In A Hero’s Journey, you’ll travel to a new realm and meet the valiant hero, Hercules from Disney’s Hercules, along with his legendary mentor, Phil! You’ll also be able to level up your experience with some highly requested improvements and an exciting new feature!

Disney Dreamlight Valley’s A Hero’s Journey free update arrives on June 3, 2026. Check out all the details below!

NEW CONTENT

To kick off the events of A Hero’s Journey, you will just need to have the Dream Castle unlocked!

  • Step through a new realm door to arrive on a training island owned by the wise satyr, Phil! But wait, this once illuminated island is severely dimmed... Could this be the Realmweaver's handywork? Train and prove you’re fit to be a hero and help illuminate the island once again!  

    [/*]

  • Join forces with Hercules to reach the gods and restore Phil’s training island back to its former glory. After displaying your heroic wits, you’ll be able to invite Herc back to your Valley, and with a little extra convincing, Phil will follow soon after.

    [/*]

  • Place Hercules’s gorgeous house and get access to this duo’s individual friendship quests and rewards!

    [/*]

  • Channel your inner deity and embrace your power in the Godly Glamor Star Path! Relish in new radiant attires and ancient furniture to create an atmosphere fit for the gods. Journey through the Star Path to unlock a Mythical Cloud Glider, Pink Baby Pegasus, Hercules’ Divine Glow Dream Style and the Olympian Cloud Mansion House Skin!

    [/*]

  • From June 10th to June 30th, the Dreamlight Parks Fest Event is back! Find and collect buttons appearing around your Valley for the chance to earn all previous, and new rewards!

    [/*]

  • Then from July 15th to July 28th, embark on the new Picture Pursuit Event! Complete daily quests from certain Villagers, locate and snap a photo of strange motifs to unlock them for personal use and collect them all for an exclusive Photo Mode frame!

    [/*]

  • The Premium Shop is getting a fresh new look, just in time for the new Hercules Bundle, featuring a new Gilded Agora Building Kit, Grecian Villa house skin and the ability to summon a fierce new ally with the new good-good-good puppy companion!

    [/*]

  • Jasmine’s loyal companion, Rajah will also be arriving in the new Cozy Companion Rajah Bundle, bringing unique interactions with Aladdin and Jasmine, Photo Mode poses, an exclusive friendship reward, and even a Tiger Cub companion!

    [/*]

  • More exciting items coming to the Premium Shop, such as an exquisite new Dream Style for Ariel, Moana’s very own parade float, and Mount accessories!

    [/*]

  • You’ll also want to keep an eye out on the Scrooge McDuck Store for brand-new items! As of this update, you’ll be able to find new clothing and furniture items each week, such as Regal Printed Skirt, Firework Waistcoat, Crown of Helios, Grecian Stone Bench, Golden Shores Suit, and more.

    [/*]

  • Seasonal Moonstone packs will also be available for a limited time.

    [/*]

Please note: Disney Dreamlight Valley Apple Arcade Edition does not include seasonal Star Paths or access to the Premium Shop.

 

IMPROVEMENTS

DREAMSNAPS

Unlimited Skips

  • As of the A Hero's Journey update, we're removing the limit on skips during DreamSnaps voting allowing you more freedom on deciding which photos you'd like to vote for!

    [/*]

DECORATING

Presets

  • Behold, an all-new way to decorate your Valley with decoration Presets! This feature works alongside Group Mode and will now allow you to select a section of your Valley and save the items placed inside as a Preset, empowering you to be able to remove, copy, and replace decorated sections with ease!

    [/*]

  • If you don’t own enough of an item to complete a saved Preset, a highlighted placeholder called a Mirage will stand in its place. You will then be able to interact with the Mirage to buy or craft the missing items and place them instantly.

    [/*]

OLAF’S GRAND EXHIBITION

New Exhibition Rooms

  • In A Hero’s Journey, Olaf’s Grand Exhibition will open two new rooms – the Forest of Valor and the Glade of Trust.

    [/*]

General Improvements

Other general improvements to the Grand Exhibition include: 

  • Transparencies for needed items on each keyhole.

    [/*]

  • Listed names for each item needed in the description section.

    [/*]

  • Tracking keyholes now observe the backpack instead of the global storage.

    [/*]

  • If a room is unreleased, there is a “Coming Soon” interaction on it.

    [/*]

PHOTO MODE

Villager Poses

  • New Photo Mode options will give you even more creative control when you want to strike a pose with your favorite Villagers!

    [/*]

  • Choose exactly where and how to place your Villagers, what attitude animations they’ll be using, and even if you want to have them paused at some point in the animation!

    [/*]

Camera Views

  • In addition, we’ve also improved the Long-range/Free Camera view to give you more flexibility to get the perfect shot!

    [/*]

MISC

Player Level

  • Level up with the new player level cap, going up from 40 to 50! Earn friendship points and progress through even more levels to unlock new exclusive fashions and furniture items.

    [/*]

Critter Schedules

  • To facilitate locating some of the rarer critters in your Valley, we’ve updated some of their schedules:

    • Squirrels: White Squirrel -> All Day (Sundays)

      [/*]

    • Rabbit: Calico Rabbit -> All Day (Thursdays)

      [/*]

    • Turtle: Black Sea Turtle-> All Day (Mondays)

      [/*]

    • Raccoon: Blue Raccoon -> All Day (Wednesdays)

      [/*]

    • Crocodile: White Crocodile -> All Day (Sundays) | Pink Crocodile -> All Day (Saturdays)

      [/*]

    • Sunbirds: Orchid Sunbird -> All Day (Fridays)

      [/*]

    • Fox: Red Fox -> All Day (Saturdays)

      [/*]

    • Raven: Brown Raven -> All Day (Tuesdays)

      [/*]

    • Monkey: Black and Gray Monkey -> All Day (Tuesdays)

      [/*]

    • Capybara: Red and White Striped Capybara -> All Day (Saturdays)

      [/*]

    • Cobra: Green and White Striped Cobra -> All Day (Thursdays)

      [/*]
    [/*]

Note: “All day” is 0:00 to 23:59 local time.

Scrooge Store

  • In this update, we’ve also improved spawning odds for items that are not owned, more diverse options between Scrooge’s Store in different villages, and better chances of seeing new items from one day to another.

    [/*]

TOP CHANGES AND BUG FIXES

  • Fixed an issue in the Grand Exhibition where sometimes the same item could be placed in two slots of a display and not be removable.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where sometimes Olaf would not appear in the Grand Exhibition.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue with Decoration Presets where the grid preview of items may be offset.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue with where the crafting menu would scroll back to the top after each crafting.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where the Edwardian Dog Bath may not be registered when added to the inventory for the “Cleanliness is Next to Dogliness" quest.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue with "The Dark Castle" quest where you might not be able to go through the door.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue in "A Fishy Dispute" quest where the spot may not be fishable. 

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue with the "An Alarming Development" quest where the Alarm Clocks are not registering as being in the inventory. 

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue in "The Decay of Fashion" where the quest is missing instructions.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue in the “Canine Cuisine" quest where the quest might not progress even after cooking the Doggy Donut.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue in the “Furniture Fluster" quest where you may not be able to pick up the red bench.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue in the "Canine Archaeology" quest where you may not be able to dig up the piece of statue in the Forgotten Lands.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where the Railway Station Ticket Booth couldn't be placed on the sides of the Railway Station Entrance.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where items couldn't be placed on top of the tracks included in the Railway Station Set.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where the game could become soft-locked after changing the Player House skin and moving the house to another biome.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where the Cozy Star Observatory house skin had flickering issues on some of its textures.

    [/*]

  • Improved the time it took to open the Customize category of the Furniture menu. 

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where items placed on the Icy Mountain River Kit couldn't be removed.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue on PlayStation where completing a Storybook Puzzle would play a repeating ticking sound.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue on PlayStation where the shortcut for auto-filling could not be used. 

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where Aurora's audio was too loud.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where Ready-to-Collect Royal Duties disappear after the section refreshes the next time you load into your game.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue with the Luminous Vivarium Wall where it would project outside lighting on the floor.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where the male Avatar would look to the side during certain actions on the horse.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where there was no UI to confirm you could duplicate a flower if you had one in the same inventory.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where the game could soft-lock after rapidly pressing the Cook button with the Skip Result Screen Option Enabled.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where the Avatars face could become distorted in preview screens when equipping certain umbrellas.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where there was a missing texture on the roof of the Scrooge McDuck’s Holiday Shop skin.

    [/*]

  • Fixed a visual issue with the cloak of the Adventuring Anna Costume.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where sunflowers in some assets would appear to be broken.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where several actions are not doable in specific radius around the Cozy Rainy House skin.

    [/*]

  • Fixed a misaligned texture issue with the Bluebird Cottager Dress.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue on the Steam Deck where the in-game keyboard was not appearing when pressing on a text field.

    [/*]

items.title: Get Ready for the Spring Floating Festival!
items.summary: Hey Valley Villagers! Can you feel that fresh breeze? The Floating Festival is returning with a new blooming edition! From April 29th, 2026, to May 19th, 2026, venture across a blossoming Floating Island to solve puzzles and claim exclusive
items.content:
Hey Valley Villagers!

Can you feel that fresh breeze? The Floating Festival is returning with a new blooming edition!

From April 29th, 2026, to May 19th, 2026, venture across a blossoming Floating Island to solve puzzles and claim exclusive seasonal rewards!

What's a Floating Festival?

A Floating Festival is an event that takes place on a specially constructed Floating Island, exclusive to the event period. This island is accessible via a portal located in the Plaza or through the event tab by clicking on the “Teleport” button.

How to Participate

A fresh celebration is in the air, and a curious portal has appeared again in the Plaza. Investigate it to be transported to a new magical place… the Spring Floating Island!

This blooming island invites you to solve its delightful mysteries. Explore each corner and sharpen your mind to tackle its puzzles!

Joining the Festival

  • Find the portal in the Plaza or click “Teleport”.

    [/*]

  • Log in regularly for new puzzles and rewards.

    [/*]

  • Some puzzles require items from your Valley – follow the clues to find them.

    [/*]

Festival Calendar Unlock Schedule

  • April 29th: Rotating Statues, Bamboo Fountain, Capybara Critter Puzzles

    [/*]

  • May 3rd: Labyrinth Puzzle

    [/*]

  • May 7th: Spring Island Quiz Puzzle 

    [/*]

  • May 11th: Escape Maze Puzzle

    [/*]

Puzzle 1: Rotating Statues Puzzle

Locate a curious quartet of statues: a seated lion, a perched raven, and a couple of graceful dancers. These elegant statues are frozen in time and spun out of place and could use a twist, or two. Find a riddle that’ll point you (and them) in the right direction!

  • Complete the puzzle to be rewarded with a majestic Horse Topiary perfect for the spring season!

    [/*]

Puzzle 2: Bamboo Fountain Puzzle

Amidst the blossoming greens, you’ll come across a broken bamboo fountain. Search the surrounding area to locate the hint for what you’ll need and set off to find the ingredients required to fix this fractured fountain.

  • You fix it, you keep it! Repair and claim your new sturdy Spring Bamboo Fountain!

    [/*]

Puzzle 3: Capybara Critter Puzzle

A picky Tinker Bell Capybara can be found wandering about the island looking for something to eat. But before feeding it, you should know this picky capy has very specific wants, and they change day-to-day. Look for a nearby hint, to help you figure out what it wants, and when it wants it! Successfully feed the capybara its favorite food to collect a memory orb!

  • Collect enough memory orbs to uncover a reward dig site. Get out your trusty shovel and collect a snazzy Embroidered Spring Apron!

    [/*]

Completed the first three puzzles? Don’t stop now – three more challenges will start on May 3rd, 7th and 11th! 

Puzzle 4: Labyrinth Puzzle

All work and no play would make anyone feel a little dull. Thankfully, here’s a twisty and twirly maze, with a reward in it! Need a hint? Try asking the Flower... 

  • The Labyrinth Puzzle will be available to enter starting May 3rd, 00:00 local time.

    [/*]

  • Find your way to a prize and claim a wonderful Frog Plushie item!

    [/*]

Puzzle 5: Spring Island Quiz Puzzle

For this puzzle, you’ll want to thoroughly explore and get acquainted with the Floating Island, as you’ll need to answer some very prickly questions... Find the Curious Flower it’s got some questions for ya!

  • The Spring Island Quiz Puzzle will be available to enter starting May 7th, 00:00 local time.

    [/*]

  • Answer all the questions correctly and receive this lovely Pixie Hollow Garden Gnome.

    [/*]

Puzzle 6: Escape Maze Puzzle

The maze hath returned! This time, the beginning is the end... is the beginning? 👈👉 Be sure to check back with the Flower before you run in (out?), you may want to check your corners...

  • The Escape Maze Puzzle will be available to enter starting May 11th, 00:00 local time.

    [/*]

  • Find your way to the end of this maze and collect the whimsy and wacky Wonderland Bird-Brella item!

    [/*]

Additional Information

See below for specifics about the Spring Floating Island:

  • Enjoy the festive decorations... with your eyes only! The island can’t be redecorated in edit mode. Additionally, some of your Royal Tools might not be available on the island, but you’ll still be able to interact with puzzle-related obstacles and some decorative elements.

    [/*]

  • Snap cheerful selfies and capture magical souvenirs! Just remember, DreamSnaps can’t be submitted while on the festival grounds.

    [/*]

  • Make the most of your time in the snowy fields! The Spring Floating Island will only be available during the event period. 

    [/*]

  • Ready to head home? Use the wishing well to exit the Floating Island or open your map for a quick return to your Valley.

    [/*]

You’re now all set to attend the festival! See you on the Spring Floating Island for some blossoming fun!

Be sure to follow our social channels for more news and updates!

  • Discord: https://gmlft.co/DDV-Discord

    [/*]

  • Instagram: https://gmlft.co/DDV-Instagram

    [/*]

  • Facebook: https://gmlft.co/DDV-Facebook

    [/*]

  • X: https://gmlft.co/DDV-X

    [/*]

  • YouTube: https://gmlft.co/DDV-YouTube

    [/*]

  • TikTok: https://gmlft.co/DDV-TikTok

    [/*]

items.title: Discover the Whispers of the Wind Update!
items.summary: Hey, Valley Villagers! Prepare for a magical adventure with the Whispers of the Wind update, now available on all platforms! Step into the breathtaking beauty of nature as you welcome Pocahontas from Disney's Pocahontas to the Valley. Follo
items.content:
Hey, Valley Villagers!

Prepare for a magical adventure with the Whispers of the Wind update, now available on all platforms!

Step into the breathtaking beauty of nature as you welcome Pocahontas from Disney's Pocahontas to the Valley. Follow the whispers of the wind, uncover hidden secrets, and embark on a heartwarming journey that leads you to Pocahontas's loyal companion, Meeko.

A Journey in the Heart of Nature

To begin your journey with Pocahontas, you will first need to have Moana as a Villager and have unlocked both the Dazzle Beach and Forest of Valor Biomes.

A gentle, mysterious wind will guide your way. Join Moana and follow the mysterious swirling leaves as they lead you toward a soft glow across the ocean, where something magical awaits. Together, you'll uncover Memories on a mysterious island tied to a rare natural event that once lit up the Valley, and meet a new friend along the way.

Bringing the Celebration to Life 

Help Pocahontas prepare a Celestial Celebration, a Valley festival honoring the Moontide Glow, a magical phenomenon that lights up the waters of Dazzle Beach.

From preparing food and crafting decorations to helping set the scene for the festival, everyone in the Valley has a role to play. Along the way, you'll deepen your bond with Pocahontas, unlock beautiful nature-inspired rewards and eventually welcome Meeko as a new companion.

A Grand Exhibition Opens

A brand-new feature coming in this update displays the magic of your Valley adventures!

Step into Olaf's Grand Exhibition, a special hub where you can showcase your collections and celebrate your achievements. At launch, you'll be able to explore rooms dedicated to the Peaceful Meadow and Dazzle Beach. Share flowers in Dioramas, fish in Aquariums, and companions in Habitats. 

Completing displays unlocks decorations you can place anywhere in your Valley, with more rooms coming in future updates.

Elements of Nature Star Path

Bring the beauty of nature into your Valley with the Elements of Nature Star Path! Collect charming furniture, whimsical nature-inspired outfits, and befriend the cute Hermit Garden Turtle.

This update also introduces Vault rewards, a new way to earn select items from past Star Paths by completing Routine Duties. Keep an eye out, as some Vault rewards will appear gradually throughout the update, and there might be a few surprises waiting for you! Read more about this new feature in our Q&A blog.

New in the Premium Shop

Add a special touch to your Valley with the new Colors of Nature Collection. Decorate Goofy's Stall and the Wishing Well, style your space with the Natural Pergola Set, and enjoy two playful swirling wind leaf effects. You can also meet a delightful new companion, Percy from Disney's Pocahontas.

Crafting Improvements

Finding what you need is easier than ever! The Crafting menu and Uncrafting Station now include handy search bars, smarter filters, and a cleaner layout to help you navigate your items quickly.

Valley Events

There are also two exciting events to enjoy during the Whispers of the Wind update. From April 29 to May 19, the Springtime Floating Festival invites you to explore festive puzzles across the Valley and earn seasonal rewards. Be sure to check out the portal in your Plaza during those days so you don't miss out!

Then, from May 27 to June 9, dig up Treasure Cracks, follow clues, and uncover hidden rewards. You can also try fishing for extra treasures in the Treasure Hunt event.

We can't wait for you to explore all the magic the Whispers of the Wind Update has to offer! Don't forget to connect with us on our official social media channels to share your in-game adventures by tagging us in your content. 

  • Instagram: https://gmlft.co/DDV-Instagram 

    [/*]

  • X: https://gmlft.co/DDV-X 

    [/*]

  • Facebook: https://gmlft.co/DDV-Facebook 

    [/*]

  • YouTube: https://gmlft.co/DDV-YouTube 

    [/*]

  • TikTok: https://gmlft.co/DDV-TikTok 

    [/*]

As always, should you run into any issues while playing Disney Dreamlight Valley, we first recommend that you check out our known issues here. If you don't see the issue you're running into, you can get in touch with our Support team via our contact form. Stumped by a certain quest or objective? Check out our friendly Discord Community for some Disney Dreamlight Valley tips and tricks!
